workers.name: narrow-catalog-permissions
workers.task:
Narrow the 23 newly-exposed BROAD GRANTs on inline catalog/inventory/purchase/finance routes. Read .orchestration/a1-erp-hy-wave7/wave7-plan.md for the full narrow perm set spec.

GOAL: Replace wide perm sets on routes with narrow perm sets that match the existing `// rbac-audit: expected-roles Owner, Admin, ...` annotations.

APPROACH:
1. For each (source, permKey, expectedRoles) triple in the plan, create a NEW narrow perm set in server/rbac/matrix.js (modeled after DealCreator / QuoteSender — single perm, isSystem: true, descriptive comment about legacy allow-list).
2. Grant the new narrow perm set to ONLY the roles in the plan's `grant_to_roles` list. Edit server/rbac/roleMatrix.js to add the new perm set to those role arrays.
3. Update the annotated route files to use `preHandler: requirePerm('<permKey>')` instead of the wide `preHandler: requireXxx(...)` helper, IF the route currently uses a wide helper. Keep routes that already use requirePerm as-is.

CONSTRAINTS:
- DO NOT remove or shrink the wide perm sets (InventoryOperator, PurchaseOperator, FinanceOperator) — other permKeys in those sets are legitimately held by these roles.
- DO NOT change role definitions for roles outside the plan's grant_to_roles list.
- DO NOT touch routes that don't have a `// rbac-audit:` annotation (leave them with their current helper, they're not in scope).
- Add a header comment on each new perm set following the Wave 5 convention (mention the legacy helper + mirror).
- Update server/app.js for any route that uses a wide preHandler but has a `// rbac-audit:` annotation. Use `requirePerm` from server/rbac/preHandler.js. Import it at the top if not already.
- DO NOT add the new perm sets to Owner/Admin via the role matrix directly — they already hold all perm sets transitively? Check: Owner holds many narrow sets explicitly listed. Add narrow sets to Owner/Admin only if the legacy allow-list included them (look at the plan's `expectedRoles` for each permKey).

VERIFICATION (must pass before committing):
1. `node scripts/lint-rbac-broad-grants.js` — broad count must drop to 0 for the routes you touched. 0 UNKNOWN. Snapshot diff must show only the routes you narrowed as newly PASS.
2. `node scripts/snapshot-catalog-grant-audit.js` to regenerate test/fixtures/catalog-grant-audit-snapshot.json
3. `node --test test/api.test.js 2>&1 | tail -5` — must show 0 fail
4. `node --test test/rbac-broad-grants.test.js test/rbac-migration.test.js 2>&1 | tail -5` — must show 0 fail
5. `node --test test/pre-existing-failures.test.js 2>&1 | tail -5` — must show 0 fail

DELIVERABLE: One commit on branch narrow-catalog-permissions titled `Wave 7: narrow {N} BROAD GRANTs on catalog/inventory/purchase routes`. Commit message body lists the new perm set IDs created. Push to origin/narrow-catalog-permissions. Write handoff.md with the verification output, new perm set names, broad count delta.

workers.name: add-inventory-adjust-perms
workers.task:
Add the missing inventory + stock adjustment permission keys that the matrix references but aren't in the catalog. Run `node scripts/lint-rbac-broad-grants.js --show-unknown 2>&1 | head -30` first to see what's already there. Then read scripts/lint-rbac-broad-grants.js to find the PERMISSIONS catalog location and any UNKNOWN log.

APPROACH:
1. Open server/rbac/permissions.js. Look for the categories section. There are inventory/stock categories. Add any perm keys referenced by InventoryOperator's permission list (inv.stock.adjust, inv.stock.scrap, inv.stock.count, inv.stock.deliver, inv.stock.transfer) and InventoryAdmin (inv.product.delete, inv.valuation.run) IF they're missing.
2. Each new entry: `{ key: 'inv.stock.adjust', category: 'inventory', description: 'Adjust stock levels' }` etc.
3. Do NOT add new role grants — these are held by roles that already have InventoryOperator / InventoryAdmin.

VERIFICATION:
1. `node scripts/lint-rbac-broad-grants.js` — UNKNOWN count must drop if you added keys
2. `node --test test/api.test.js 2>&1 | tail -5` — 0 fail
3. `node --test test/rbac-broad-grants.test.js 2>&1 | tail -5` — 0 fail

DELIVERABLE: One commit on branch add-inventory-adjust-perms titled `Wave 7: register missing inventory adjustment perm keys`. Push to origin. Write handoff.md.

workers.name: extract-purchase-narrow-sets
workers.task:
Extract the purchase-vendor and purchase-po narrow perm sets that narrow-catalog-permissions might not have done. Read .orchestration/a1-erp-hy-wave7/wave7-plan.md to see the exact perm keys in scope for this worker: `purchase.vendor.read`, `purchase.po.read`, `purchase.analytics.read`, `purchase.vendor.create`, `purchase.po.create`, `purchase.po.update`, `purchase.receipt.create`, `purchase.return.create`, `finance.bill.create`.

GOAL: Create narrow perm sets `PurchaseVendorReader`, `PurchaseOrderReader`, `PurchaseAnalyticsReader`, `PurchaseVendorWriter`, `PurchaseOrderWriter`, `PurchaseReceiptWriter`, `PurchaseReturnWriter`, `FinanceBillWriter`. Each holds ONE perm key (modeled after DealCreator / QuoteSender).

APPROACH:
1. Add the 8 new perm sets to server/rbac/matrix.js under a `Wave 7 narrow grant sets` header comment, mirroring the Wave 5 header comment pattern.
2. Grant each to the role set that matches the legacy `// rbac-audit: expected-roles` annotation. Use roleMatrix.js to add them. Most should go to: Owner, Admin, Accountant, Auditor, Operator, FinanceLead, InventoryLead, PurchaseLead, Purchaser. VendorPortal holds `purchase.vendor.read` and `purchase.po.read` only.
3. In server/app.js, replace the wide preHandler on the matching route (e.g. POST /api/purchase/orders uses `preHandler: requirePerm('purchase.po.create')` if it currently uses a wide helper). Routes that already use requirePerm need no change.

CONSTRAINTS:
- VendorPortal should only get `PurchaseVendorReader` and `PurchaseOrderReader` (read-only).
- Don't touch narrow-catalog-permissions's perm sets — there may be overlap on `inv.product.read` but they use DIFFERENT perm set names (Worker A created `CatalogReader`, you create `PurchaseVendorReader` etc. — disjoint scopes).
- If you find a route that Worker A already converted to requirePerm, skip it.

VERIFICATION:
1. `node scripts/lint-rbac-broad-grants.js` — broad count drops by 8 (purchase routes narrowed)
2. `node scripts/snapshot-catalog-grant-audit.js`
3. `node --test test/api.test.js 2>&1 | tail -5` — 0 fail
4. `node --test test/rbac-broad-grants.test.js 2>&1 | tail -5` — 0 fail

DELIVERABLE: One commit on branch extract-purchase-narrow-sets titled `Wave 7: 8 narrow purchase perm sets + 8 BROAD GRANTs → PASS`. Push. Write handoff.md.
